title: Eurybia integrifolia
taxonomy: Eurybia integrifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the integrifolium sea lavender or integrifolium sea rose. It is native to the coastal areas of the northern hemisphere, particularly in the regions of eastern North America and western Europe. Within the family Asteraceae, Eurybia integrifolia is part of the subfamily Asteroideae, which comprises many species of daisies and sunflowers. Further classification places it in the tribe Astereae, a group of plants commonly known as the aster tribe. The genus Eurybia is a small group of plants that are often confused with other genera within the family Asteraceae.
uses: Eurybia integrifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine, although the efficacy of these uses has not been extensively scientifically proven. It is often employed in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and roots are said to possess medicinal properties, although the exact compounds responsible for these effects are not well understood. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's potential medicinal uses and their safety for human consumption.
